other_observers: I overlapped a bit with a CFO convention field trip group returning from this area including David Leatherman, Joel & Marcel Such, et al.;
specific_location: Devil's Kitchen area, Colorado National Monument
description: Long-tailed, overall gray sparrow with brownish upperparts & pink bill offset by black front of face & black throat. Very distinctive plumage and very close looks- unmistakeable ID.Behaviors: Bird 1 was singing from prominent perches. At one point this bird flew to a bush where another BCSP was present. The interaction didn't seem too aggressive and the other bird wasn't hear singing- perhaps a female?Call: at least one of the two birds was a male- it was singing the species' typical accelerating, descending trill with an upslurred end. Video including several repetitions of the song here: http://tinyurl.com/BCCH-20May2011Plumage: Adult
photographer: Photographer: Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker
